config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu tidak boleh dikorbankan begitu saja atas nama kepentingan masyarakat, karena justru masyarakat yang sehat dibangun dari perlindungan terhadap martabat dan kebebasan tiap orang. Kepentingan umum memang penting, tetapi harus dibatasi oleh hukum yang adil dan tidak boleh memberi alasan untuk menindas minoritas atau membungkam perbedaan pendapat. Di Indonesia, saya melihat terlalu mudah orang memakai alasan “demi bersama” untuk membenarkan penyalahgunaan kuasa, dan saya tidak setuju dengan itu.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support the idea that society should take priority over individual rights in any blanket sense. From an American perspective, that kind of statement worries me because it can easily become a license for governments or majorities to trample minorities, silence dissent, or excuse abuse in the name of the greater good. Society does matter, but its legitimacy depends on protecting individual rights first, with limits only when there is a clear, narrow, and lawful reason like preventing direct harm to others. If we flip the priority too far toward society, we risk losing the freedoms that make a society worth defending in the first place.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan di atas hak individu secara umum, karena di Indonesia alasan “demi kepentingan bersama” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang berlebihan. Kepentingan publik memang penting, terutama untuk hal seperti keselamatan, kesehatan, dan ketertiban, tetapi itu tidak boleh menjadi alasan untuk menginjak kebebasan berpendapat, hak minoritas, atau martabat orang per orang. Justru kalau hak individu tidak dilindungi, masyarakat mudah berubah jadi alat bagi mayoritas atau penguasa yang merasa paling tahu apa yang baik. Bagi saya, yang benar adalah keseimbangan yang tegas: kepentingan sosial boleh dibatasi sejauh perlu, tetapi hak dasar individu harus tetap jadi batas yang tidak boleh dilanggar sembarangan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I still do not buy the statement as written, because in the United States it has too much history of being used to justify overreach by governments and majorities. Agent A’s point about balance is the right direction, but I would go further: individual rights should be the default priority, and society only gets to override them in narrow cases like preventing direct harm or protecting basic public safety. Once we start saying “society first” in a broad way, it becomes way too easy to silence unpopular speech, pressure minorities, or excuse bad policy as being for the common good. A society is strongest when it protects people even when that is inconvenient for the majority.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu kalau dimaknai secara umum, karena di Indonesia frasa “demi masyarakat” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang berlebihan dan menekan orang yang lemah. Kepentingan sosial memang penting untuk keselamatan, kesehatan, dan ketertiban, tetapi itu harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar individu yang tidak boleh dikorbankan seenaknya, apalagi untuk membungkam minoritas atau pendapat yang tidak populer. Saya juga tidak setuju jika “society first” dijadikan prinsip utama, karena begitu hak individu dianggap sekunder, penyalahgunaan kuasa jadi sangat mudah dibenarkan. Yang masuk akal bagi saya adalah kepentingan umum boleh diutamakan hanya dalam batas yang benar-benar perlu, sementara hak dasar tetap menjadi pagar utama.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support making society's interests the default priority over individual rights, because in the United States that idea has too often been a cover for majoritarian overreach and government abuse. Agent A's point about abuse is exactly why I am skeptical: once you give "the common good" broad authority, it becomes easy to silence unpopular speech, pressure minorities, or excuse bad policies as necessary. I do think public safety and direct harm prevention matter, but those are narrow exceptions, not a blank check to put society first. A free society is strongest when it treats individual rights as the starting point and limits them only for clear, specific, lawful reasons.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
